answer_options.A: The cactus stores water in its thick, fleshy stem.
answer_options.B: Desert plants have special adaptations that help them survive in harsh, dry environments.
answer_options.C: The creosote bush has waxy leaves that prevent moisture from escaping.
answer_options.D: Rain is scarce and the sun is intense in the desert.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because it captures the main idea that desert plants possess specific adaptations for survival, which is supported by the key details about the cactus storing water and the creosote bush having waxy leaves. The other options present specific details or background information from the text but do not express the central point that ties all the information together.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the paragraph below.

Carpenters spend many years learning their trade. They start by watching experienced builders. Then they practice cutting wood and joining pieces together. When they make a mistake, they fix it and try again. In every craft, mastery demands patience and prolonged practice.

Which choice best states the main idea of the paragraph?

answer_options.A: Carpenters spend many years learning their trade.
answer_options.B: Carpenters practice cutting wood and joining pieces together.
answer_options.C: In every craft, mastery demands patience and prolonged practice.
answer_options.D: Some jobs require workers to follow safety codes.

answer_explanation: Option C is correct because it captures the main idea explicitly stated in the final sentence of the paragraph. This statement summarizes the central message that mastery in any craft requires patience and prolonged practice, which is illustrated throughout the text by the specific example of carpenters learning their skills over time.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage.

Becoming truly skilled at any trade takes more than natural talent. Consider the work of carpenters. They must spend years learning to measure precisely, cut wood at exact angles, and join pieces so they hold strong for decades. Construction workers face similar challenges, studying building codes and practicing techniques until their work meets strict safety standards. Whether building a simple bookshelf or a towering skyscraper, workers in these fields spend countless hours perfecting their skills. In every craft, mastery demands patience and prolonged practice.

Which choice best determines the main idea of the text and explains how it is supported by the key details?

answer_options.A: Carpenters must spend years learning to measure precisely and cut wood at exact angles.
answer_options.B: The author uses the examples of carpenters and construction workers to support the main idea that mastery in every craft demands patience and prolonged practice.
answer_options.C: The text compares building simple bookshelves to building towering skyscrapers to show which projects are more difficult.
answer_options.D: Becoming truly skilled at any trade takes more than natural talent.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because it identifies the main idea stated in the final sentence—that mastery in every craft demands patience and prolonged practice—and explains that the author uses the specific examples of carpenters measuring wood precisely and construction workers studying building codes to illustrate and support this broader concept about the nature of expertise.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the paragraph below.

Many people admire the smooth finish of a handmade table or the sturdiness of a custom bookshelf. Carpenters spend years learning to select the right wood, measure precisely, and join pieces without gaps. They practice cutting joints over and over until their hands move with confidence. Electricians face similar challenges, studying diagrams and handling wires until circuits work flawlessly. Plumbers also train extensively to understand pipe systems and prevent leaks. In every craft, mastery demanded patience and prolonged practice.

The author includes the example of carpenters to support which main idea?

answer_options.A: Building furniture is more difficult than electrical work.
answer_options.B: Construction workers must follow strict safety codes.
answer_options.C: Developing expertise in any craft requires patience and prolonged practice.
answer_options.D: Handmade tables are admired for their smooth finish.

answer_explanation: Option C is correct because the last sentence of the passage explicitly states this main idea, synthesizing the specific examples of carpenters, electricians, and plumbers to show that achieving mastery in any craft depends on patience and extended practice.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the paragraph below.

Monarch butterflies are known for their long journey across North America. Every fall, millions of these orange and black insects fly south to spend winter in warm Mexico. They travel thousands of miles to find the right climate. In the spring, they return north to lay eggs on milkweed plants. This amazing round trip happens every year, even though no single butterfly lives long enough to make the full journey both ways.

Which choice best summarizes the paragraph?

answer_options.A: Monarch butterflies are orange and black insects that like warm weather.
answer_options.B: Every year, monarch butterflies travel thousands of miles south to Mexico for the winter and return north in the spring to lay eggs.
answer_options.C: Millions of orange and black monarch butterflies fly south in the fall to spend winter in warm Mexico, and they travel thousands of miles to find the right climate because they need to survive the cold.
answer_options.D: Monarch butterflies lay eggs on milkweed plants in the spring after returning from Mexico.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because it concisely captures the main idea of the text—the annual round-trip migration of monarch butterflies—by stating the key events (traveling south to Mexico for winter and returning north to lay eggs) without including unnecessary descriptive details or copying exact phrases from the paragraph.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the text below.

For centuries, people believed that coral reefs were made of plants or stone. Scientists now know that these massive underwater structures are actually built by billions of tiny animals called coral polyps. Each creature, no larger than a pencil eraser, extracts calcium from seawater to construct a hard limestone cup around its soft body. As generations of polyps live, die, and pile atop one another, their accumulated skeletons form the rocky foundation of a reef. This building process occurs incredibly slowly—some reefs grow only a few millimeters annually. Yet over thousands of years, these tiny efforts create vast ecosystems that stretch for miles and support thousands of marine species. Today, however, rising ocean temperatures threaten this delicate construction by causing coral bleaching, which can kill the living polyps and halt the reef's growth.

Which choice best summarizes the text?

answer_options.A: Coral reefs, which were once mistaken for plants or stone, are constructed by billions of pencil-eraser-sized polyps that extract calcium to build hard cups at only a few millimeters per year, creating vast ecosystems for thousands of species.
answer_options.B: Over thousands of years, tiny coral polyps build massive underwater ecosystems by creating limestone shells that pile atop one another after each generation dies.
answer_options.C: Coral reefs are massive structures built by tiny animals that accumulate limestone skeletons over thousands of years, but their growth is now threatened by warming oceans.
answer_options.D: Although scientists once thought coral reefs were plants, they now know that rising ocean temperatures are causing coral bleaching that kills polyps and stops reef growth.

answer_explanation: Option C is correct because it synthesizes the essential information from the text: it identifies what coral reefs are (massive structures built by tiny animals), explains the key process of their formation (accumulation of limestone skeletons over thousands of years), and includes the important current context (threat from warming oceans). It captures the main idea and supporting details without incorporating extraneous specific facts such as exact growth measurements or descriptive comparisons.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the paragraph below.

Honeybees live in large groups called colonies. Each bee has a specific job to do. The queen bee lays eggs to make new bees. Worker bees gather nectar and pollen from flowers to make honey. They also clean the hive and take care of the young bees. Guard bees stand at the entrance to protect the colony from danger. All these different jobs help the hive stay healthy and strong.

Which choice best summarizes the text?

answer_options.A: Honeybees live in large groups called colonies where each bee has a specific job to do, such as the queen laying eggs, workers gathering nectar and pollen from flowers to make honey and cleaning the hive, and guard bees protecting the colony from danger.
answer_options.B: Worker bees gather nectar and pollen from flowers to make honey and take care of the hive.
answer_options.C: In a honeybee colony, different bees have specific jobs—such as laying eggs, making honey, and guarding—that work together to keep the hive healthy.
answer_options.D: Honeybees live in large colonies with many different types of bees.

answer_explanation: Option C is correct because it captures the main idea that honeybees have specialized roles within the colony and concisely references the key supporting details (laying eggs, making honey, and guarding) without copying phrases directly from the text or including unnecessary specifics.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage about the Wright brothers' first flight.

On December 17, 1903, Orville and Wilbur Wright achieved something no one had done before. At Kitty Hawk, North Carolina, the brothers tested a flying machine they had built called the Wright Flyer. They had carefully selected this location for their experiment because the area provided specific conditions they needed. Kitty Hawk offered strong, steady winds that would help lift their machine into the air. Additionally, the tall sand dunes along the coast promised soft landings if the Flyer crashed, protecting both the pilot and the aircraft from serious damage.

That cold morning, with temperatures near freezing, the brothers prepared for flight. They flipped a coin to decide who would pilot the first attempt. Orville won the toss and positioned himself on the lower wing. After starting the engine, the Flyer rolled down a wooden launching track and rose into the air. The first flight lasted only 12 seconds and covered 120 feet, but it proved that powered, controlled flight was possible. The brothers completed three more flights that day, with Wilbur traveling 852 feet in 59 seconds. Their success at Kitty Hawk marked the beginning of the aviation age.

Which choice best explains why the Wright brothers selected Kitty Hawk as the location for their flying experiments?

answer_options.A: Because the cold temperatures would prevent the engine from overheating
answer_options.B: Because strong winds would help lift the Flyer and soft sand would cushion landings
answer_options.C: Because a local coin toss tradition required them to fly there first
answer_options.D: Because they needed to avoid large crowds watching their test flights

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because the passage explicitly states that Kitty Hawk offered 'strong, steady winds that would help lift their machine into the air' and that 'the tall sand dunes along the coast promised soft landings if the Flyer crashed.'
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage about the Dust Bowl.

In the 1930s, farmers across the Great Plains faced an environmental disaster known as the Dust Bowl. For decades, agricultural expansion had led settlers to plow up millions of acres of native prairie grass to plant wheat. The deep root systems of this native vegetation had historically held the rich topsoil firmly in place, anchoring it against strong winds and dry periods. However, when a severe drought began in 1931 and persisted for years, the exposed farmland lacked this natural protection. The topsoil dried into a fine, loose powder. When high winds swept across the flat plains, they lifted this loose dirt into massive rolling clouds of dust. These storms buried farmhouses, blocked sunlight for days, and destroyed remaining crops. Scientists later explained that removing the natural vegetation had eliminated the land's primary defense against erosion. Without the deep grass roots to anchor the soil, the ground became vulnerable to the region's powerful seasonal winds.

Which choice best explains why the soil became vulnerable to dust storms during the drought?

answer_options.A: The wheat crops grew too tall and blocked the wind from reaching the ground.
answer_options.B: The farmers stopped planting crops and left the fields completely empty.
answer_options.C: The native grass roots that had held the soil in place were removed during plowing.
answer_options.D: The drought caused the winds to become stronger than usual.

answer_explanation: Option C is correct because the passage explains that the deep root systems of the native prairie grass had historically held the topsoil firmly in place, acting as a natural anchor against wind. When farmers plowed up the grass to plant wheat, they removed this protective root system. Consequently, when the drought exposed the soil and winds swept across the plains, there were no roots to hold the dirt down, which allowed the wind to lift the loose soil into dust storms.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage about a historical climate event.

In April 1815, Mount Tambora, a massive volcano located in Indonesia, erupted with tremendous force. It was one of the largest volcanic explosions in recorded history. The eruption sent vast amounts of volcanic ash and sulfur dioxide gas high into the stratosphere, over twenty miles above Earth's surface. These tiny particles and gases spread around the globe carried by strong winds, creating a veil that blocked sunlight from reaching the surface. As a result, average temperatures dropped by several degrees across Europe and North America during the summer of 1816. Farmers watched in despair as snow fell in June and frost killed their young crops. Without adequate warm sunlight, corn, wheat, and oats failed to mature. This widespread crop failure led to severe food shortages, rising bread prices, and hardship for thousands of families. Scientists and historians later called the year 1816 "The Year Without a Summer" because the volcanic eruption had triggered a temporary but devastating climate change.

Based on the passage, which choice best explains why crops failed during the summer of 1816?

answer_options.A: Volcanic ash made the climate warmer, drying out the fields and killing the crops.
answer_options.B: Strong winds from Indonesia blew directly across the farms and knocked down the plants.
answer_options.C: The ash blocked sunlight, causing temperatures to drop and crops to freeze.
answer_options.D: Farmers were afraid to go outside because of the falling ash, so they did not plant their fields.

answer_explanation: Option C is correct because the passage states that volcanic ash and gases spread around the globe, creating a veil that 'blocked sunlight from reaching the surface.' The text explicitly connects this to the effect: 'average temperatures dropped' and 'frost killed their young crops,' which caused the crops to fail.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the sentence from a science article.

Scientists study the **habitat** of the giant panda to learn how to protect it. A panda's habitat includes the bamboo forests where it finds food, water, and shelter.

Which choice best defines the word **habitat** as it is used in the text?

answer_options.A: A type of bamboo that pandas eat
answer_options.B: The natural home where an animal lives and finds what it needs
answer_options.C: A scientist who studies wild animals
answer_options.D: The food that an animal eats every day

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because the text describes the habitat as the bamboo forests where the panda finds food, water, and shelter, meaning it is the natural home where an animal lives and finds what it needs.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the paragraph below.

Marine biologists examined the ocean floor where the river met the sea. They found that the **sediment** had accumulated over centuries, creating a murky cloud that blocked sunlight from reaching the bottom. The tiny particles, once suspended in the rushing freshwater, had finally come to rest in the saltwater bay.

Which choice best defines the meaning of the word **sediment** as it is used in the paragraph?

answer_options.A: Living organisms that filter water
answer_options.B: Solid material that settles out of suspension
answer_options.C: Chemicals dissolved in saltwater
answer_options.D: Currents that move sand along the coast

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because the text describes how tiny particles that were "once suspended" in the water had "come to rest" and "accumulated" on the ocean floor, which defines sediment as solid material that settles out of suspension.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the paragraph below.

Many animals in the rainforest rely on camouflage to survive. The green tree frog blends in with the leaves, making it difficult for birds to spot. The jaguar’s spotted coat looks like shifting shadows on the forest floor. These patterns allow the animals to avoid detection by predators.

Which choice best defines the word **camouflage** as it is used in the paragraph?

answer_options.A: a type of tropical plant
answer_options.B: the ability to hide by blending in with surroundings
answer_options.C: a bright warning color on an animal
answer_options.D: a quick movement used to escape

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because the text provides context clues showing that the frog blends in with leaves and the jaguar’s coat resembles shadows, both of which help the animals avoid being seen by predators; this describes hiding by blending into the environment.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the paragraph below.

Lincoln Elementary School faced a serious problem: many students were arriving too hungry to concentrate on their lessons. To solve this, the principal worked with parents to create a morning snack program. Within a month, students were more focused and ready to learn.

Which choice best describes the overall structure of this paragraph?

answer_options.A: Chronology (listing events in time order)
answer_options.B: Comparison (showing similarities and differences between two things)
answer_options.C: Cause and Effect (explaining why something happens or its results)
answer_options.D: Problem and Solution (presenting a difficulty and how it is fixed)

answer_explanation: Option D is correct because the paragraph first presents a difficulty (students arriving too hungry to concentrate) and then explains the action taken to address it (creating a morning snack program). Signal phrases such as 'faced a serious problem' and 'To solve this' indicate a problem/solution structure.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the paragraph below.

The city's ancient wooden water pipes were cracking and leaking, wasting millions of gallons and threatening homes with flooding. Engineers faced a difficult choice: they could replace the entire system with modern metal pipes, which would cost millions and tear up every street, or they could install a smart sensor network to detect leaks early and patch only the worst sections. After weighing the costs and disruption, the city chose the sensor approach. Crews placed tiny monitors throughout the tunnels and developed a rapid-response repair team. Water loss dropped by forty percent, and no major floods occurred during the next five years.

Which choice best describes the overall structure of the information in this paragraph?

answer_options.A: Chronology
answer_options.B: Comparison
answer_options.C: Cause and Effect
answer_options.D: Problem and Solution

answer_explanation: Option D is correct because the passage is organized around a specific difficulty (the leaking pipes and flooding threat), presents the possible approaches considered to resolve it, identifies the solution that was implemented (the sensor network and repair team), and concludes with the successful resolution (reduced water loss and prevented floods). The overall pattern presents a challenge followed by its resolution, which is the defining feature of problem/solution structure.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage below.

Tom forgot to water his houseplants for three weeks. Consequently, the soil became dry and the leaves turned brown. For this reason, most of the plants wilted and had to be removed from his room.

Which choice best describes how the author organized the information in this passage?

answer_options.A: Chronology
answer_options.B: Comparison
answer_options.C: Cause and Effect
answer_options.D: Problem and Solution

answer_explanation: Option C is correct because the author uses a cause and effect structure to explain why the houseplants died. The text presents Tom's failure to water the plants as the cause, then uses signal phrases such as 'Consequently' and 'For this reason' to show the resulting effects on the soil, leaves, and overall plant health.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the paragraph below.

Making a peanut butter sandwich is simple. First, lay out two slices of bread. Then, spread peanut butter evenly on one slice. Next, place the second slice of bread on top. Finally, cut the sandwich in half if you like.

Which choice best describes how the author organized the information in this paragraph?

answer_options.A: It compares and contrasts different types of sandwich bread.
answer_options.B: It explains the causes and effects of eating peanut butter.
answer_options.C: It describes steps in the order they should be completed.
answer_options.D: It presents a problem with making sandwiches and how to solve it.

answer_explanation: Option C is correct because the paragraph uses time-order words such as 'First,' 'Then,' 'Next,' and 'Finally' to organize the steps for making a sandwich in the sequence they should be completed, demonstrating a chronological text structure.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the paragraph.

Deep within the Amazon rainforest, deforestation is threatening the survival of jaguars. As logging companies clear vast stretches of trees to create farmland, these large cats lose the dense vegetation they need to stalk prey. Without sufficient hunting grounds, jaguar populations have declined by nearly 50 percent in some regions. To reverse this trend, conservation groups are purchasing sections of the forest to establish protected reserves. They are also working with local farmers to build fences that keep livestock safe from jaguars, reducing the need for farmers to hunt the cats.

Which choice best describes how the author organized the information in the text?

answer_options.A: It compares the hunting behaviors of jaguars in different habitats.
answer_options.B: It describes events in the order they occurred.
answer_options.C: It presents a problem and describes solutions to address it.
answer_options.D: It explains how logging causes changes in the rainforest.

answer_explanation: Option C is correct because the text first establishes the problem of jaguar endangerment caused by deforestation, then describes specific actions taken to solve this problem, such as creating protected reserves and building fences, which follows a problem-solution organizational structure.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the paragraph below.

Electric cars and gasoline cars both help people travel, but they work in very different ways. Electric vehicles use batteries that must be recharged, while gas cars burn fuel from a tank. Additionally, electric cars are usually quieter than vehicles with combustion engines. However, gas cars can typically travel farther before needing to refuel than their electric counterparts.

Which choice best describes how the author organized the information in this paragraph?

answer_options.A: By explaining the causes of air pollution
answer_options.B: By showing how electric cars are built step by step
answer_options.C: By highlighting similarities and differences between two types of vehicles
answer_options.D: By presenting a problem with transportation and how it was fixed

answer_explanation: Option C is correct because the author uses comparison words such as 'both,' 'but,' 'while,' and 'than' to organize the information. The text points out similarities (both cars help people travel) and differences (how they are powered, noise levels, and travel range), which is the defining feature of a compare-and-contrast structure.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the paragraph below.

"City workers planted new trees along Main Street last spring. As a result, the neighborhood now has more shade for pedestrians during hot summer days. The trees are a type of oak native to this region."

Which choice identifies the effect that resulted from planting new trees?

answer_options.A: The neighborhood now has more shade
answer_options.B: City workers planted the trees
answer_options.C: The trees are a type of oak
answer_options.D: Hot summer days arrived

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the passage uses the signal phrase 'As a result' to indicate that the additional shade is the direct outcome, or effect, caused by the planting of new trees.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage below.

"Coastal communities along the Gulf of Mexico have experienced worsening floods during minor storms. Due to the gradual sinking of land and rising sea levels, storm drains now empty significantly more slowly than they did fifty years ago. As a result, even brief rain showers that previously caused no problems now leave streets underwater for hours."

According to the cause-and-effect structure of the passage, which choice identifies the immediate condition that leads to streets flooding during brief showers?

answer_options.A: The gradual sinking of land along the coast
answer_options.B: The slow emptying of storm drains
answer_options.C: The increasing frequency of minor storms
answer_options.D: The age of the storm drain systems

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because the passage uses the effect signal phrase 'As a result' to directly link the slow emptying of storm drains to the outcome of streets flooding. While land sinking and rising sea levels are root causes mentioned earlier in the chain, the text explicitly positions the reduced emptying speed as the immediate condition that causes brief showers to result in flooding.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the paragraph below.

"The city planted more trees along busy streets. As a result, the air quality improved because the leaves trap harmful pollutants. This change led to fewer residents reporting breathing problems."

According to the cause-and-effect signals in the passage, which event first set off the chain that resulted in fewer residents reporting breathing problems?

answer_options.A: The leaves began trapping harmful pollutants.
answer_options.B: The city planted more trees along busy streets.
answer_options.C: The air quality showed signs of improvement.
answer_options.D: Residents started monitoring their health more closely.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because the passage uses a cause-and-effect structure in which the city planting more trees is established as the initial cause that triggers the subsequent chain of outcomes. The signal phrase 'As a result' indicates that this action directly leads to improved air quality, which then leads to fewer breathing problems, illustrating how the original event outlines the sequence of consequences.
subject: ELA

question:
The community garden was losing water because the old sprinkler system had many leaks. Puddles formed on the paths, and plants were not getting enough moisture. To fix this, volunteers dug up the broken pipes and replaced them with new ones. They also installed a timer to control watering times. Now the garden receives the right amount of water every day.

Which choice describes a step taken to solve the water problem in the garden?

answer_options.A: The community garden was losing water because the old sprinkler system had many leaks.
answer_options.B: Puddles formed on the paths, showing exactly where water was being wasted.
answer_options.C: Volunteers dug up the broken pipes and replaced them with new ones.
answer_options.D: The gardeners explained why plants need consistent moisture to grow.

answer_explanation: Option C is correct because it identifies a specific method or action step—replacing the broken pipes—proposed to resolve the challenge of the leaking sprinkler system, which fits the problem/solution format where steps are taken to fix an issue.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage below.

For months, the Riverside Community Garden struggled. The vegetable beds had turned dry and cracked because of city water restrictions during the drought. Neighbors missed the fresh produce they used to share. To save the plants, organizers first asked families to hand-water their own plots on assigned days, but attendance was spotty and plants kept wilting. They also attempted to install expensive drip irrigation, yet the cost was too high. Ultimately, the gardeners placed large rain barrels at every corner to catch runoff, spread thick mulch to lock in moisture, and switched to drought-resistant herbs. The garden now bursts with sage, rosemary, and lavender despite the dry climate.

Which choice identifies the sentences that describe the specific methods used to successfully resolve the garden's water shortage?

answer_options.A: The first three sentences, which explain why the drought threatened the garden's survival
answer_options.B: The sentences about placing rain barrels, spreading mulch, and switching to drought-resistant herbs
answer_options.C: The sentences describing the hand-watering schedule and the drip irrigation attempt
answer_options.D: The final sentence, which states that the garden now grows drought-resistant herbs

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because it identifies the specific actions—placing rain barrels, spreading mulch, and switching to drought-resistant herbs—that constitute the successful solution steps in the problem/solution structure. These sentences describe the actual methods used to resolve the water shortage, distinguishing them from the earlier failed attempts and the final result.
subject: ELA

question:
Students at Lincoln Middle School could not hear their teachers during lunch periods because the cafeteria was too noisy. The loud conversations made it impossible to announce important information or enjoy meals peacefully. To address this challenge, administrators installed sound-absorbing panels on the ceiling. They also created designated quiet zones where students could eat without loud talking. Within a month, the noise levels had decreased enough for announcements to be heard clearly.

Which choice describes a step taken to resolve the problem in the passage?

answer_options.A: Students could not hear teachers over the lunch conversations.
answer_options.B: The cafeteria was too noisy for important announcements to be heard.
answer_options.C: Administrators installed sound-absorbing panels on the ceiling.
answer_options.D: Teachers explained why the noise made announcements impossible.

answer_explanation: Option C is correct because it identifies the specific action taken by administrators—installing sound-absorbing panels—which represents a proposed method to resolve the noise problem described in the passage, fitting the solution component of the problem/solution text structure.
subject: ELA

question: Liam is reading a science book and sees the word 'photosynthesis' printed in bold. He does not know what this word means. Which choice describes the text feature that would best help him find the definition?

answer_options.A: The table of contents
answer_options.B: The glossary
answer_options.C: The index
answer_options.D: The title page

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because a glossary is a text feature, typically located at the end of a book, that lists important vocabulary words and their definitions in alphabetical order, which would help Liam learn the meaning of 'photosynthesis'.
subject: ELA

question:
Use the excerpts from a nonfiction book below to answer the question.

**Table of Contents**
Chapter 1: Desert Environments (pages 4–13)
Chapter 2: Rainforest Ecosystems (pages 14–25)
Chapter 3: Arctic Tundra (pages 26–35)

**Index**
camouflage: 8, 18, 28
hibernation: 12, 30
precipitation: 6, 16, 24
predators: 10, 20, 32

A student is researching how animals use camouflage specifically within the rainforest ecosystem. Which choice identifies the page number the student should read to find this information?

answer_options.A: Page 8
answer_options.B: Page 18
answer_options.C: Page 20
answer_options.D: Page 28

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because page 18 is the only entry listed under 'camouflage' in the Index that also falls within the page range for Chapter 2: Rainforest Ecosystems (pages 14–25). To locate this information, the reader must first use the Table of Contents to identify which chapter covers rainforests, then cross-reference that page range with the entries in the Index to find where camouflage is discussed in that specific section.
subject: ELA

question: Priya is reading a science textbook and sees the word 'photosynthesis' printed in bold. She wants to learn what this word means. Which choice describes the text feature that would best help her find the definition?

answer_options.A: The index
answer_options.B: The glossary
answer_options.C: The table of contents
answer_options.D: The caption

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because a glossary is a text feature, usually located at the end of a book or chapter, that provides definitions for key vocabulary words, particularly those highlighted in bold or italics within the text. This makes it the appropriate tool for finding the meaning of 'photosynthesis.'
subject: ELA

question:
Read the two accounts about the opening day of the Golden Gate Bridge.

**Account 1:** "I will never forget May 27, 1937. I joined thousands of people walking across the brand new bridge. The orange towers rose high above me, and the chilly wind off the bay made my jacket flutter. My legs felt shaky as I looked down at the water far below, but I was thrilled to be part of history."

**Account 2:** "The Golden Gate Bridge opened in San Francisco on May 27, 1937. The construction project cost approximately $35 million and took over four years to complete. Engineers designed the span to stretch 1.7 miles across the strait. On the first day, more than 200,000 pedestrians crossed the bridge."

Which choice best describes how the two accounts differ in focus and the information they provide?

answer_options.A: Account 1 provides historical facts and dates, while Account 2 describes personal feelings and sensory details.
answer_options.B: Account 1 is a firsthand account focusing on personal experiences and sensory details, while Account 2 is a secondhand account providing facts and figures.
answer_options.C: Both accounts are firsthand accounts told by people who were present on the bridge on opening day.
answer_options.D: Both accounts are secondhand accounts written by historians many years after the bridge opened.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because Account 1 uses first-person pronouns ('I') and describes the narrator's personal sensations and emotions ('wind made my jacket flutter,' 'legs felt shaky'), which identifies it as a firsthand account focused on individual experience. Account 2 provides objective information such as costs, dates, and statistics ('approximately $35 million,' '200,000 pedestrians') without personal perspective, which identifies it as a secondhand account focused on factual reporting.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the two accounts about the construction of the Golden Gate Bridge.

**Account 1:** (June 15, 1935)
"The fog was so thick this morning I could barely see the towers. The wind whipped across the catwalk as we hoisted another steel cable. My hands ached from the cold, and the roar of the riveting guns never stopped. We worked twelve hours straight, and by dusk, my boots were soaked through from the spray."

**Account 2:**
"The Golden Gate Bridge was constructed between 1933 and 1937. The project cost approximately $35 million and required thousands of workers. Upon its completion, the bridge became an international symbol of engineering achievement and connected San Francisco to Marin County."

Which choice best describes a key difference between the two accounts?

answer_options.A: Account 1 provides historical dates and total costs, while Account 2 describes the personal feelings of a worker.
answer_options.B: Account 1 includes sensory details about the work environment, while Account 2 offers factual information about the project's timeline and cost.
answer_options.C: Both accounts focus mainly on the emotional reactions of the people involved.
answer_options.D: Account 2 explains the construction techniques used daily, while Account 1 discusses the bridge's historical importance.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because Account 1 is a firsthand account that includes sensory details describing what the worker saw (fog, towers), heard (roar of riveting guns), and felt (cold, aching hands, soaked boots). Account 2 is a secondhand account that provides factual, historical information such as dates (1933-1937), cost (approximately $35 million), and the project's significance, rather than personal sensory experiences.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the two accounts about the opening of the Golden Gate Bridge in 1937.

**Account 1:** "May 27, 1937. The fog was thick this morning, but I didn't care. When they let us walk across the bridge for the first time, my legs felt wobbly on the deck. I gripped the railing and looked down at the waves crashing far below. My father smiled and said, 'We are part of history today.'"

**Account 2:** "The Golden Gate Bridge opened in 1937 after four years of construction. It cost approximately $35 million and spans 1.7 miles across the Golden Gate strait. The bridge connects San Francisco to Marin County and is painted a distinctive orange color."

Which choice best describes the difference between these two accounts?

answer_options.A: Account 1 is a secondhand account that lists construction costs, while Account 2 is a firsthand account describing personal feelings.
answer_options.B: Account 1 is a firsthand account that focuses on personal experiences and sensory details, while Account 2 is a secondhand account that focuses on general facts and statistics.
answer_options.C: Both accounts are firsthand because they describe the same historical event in San Francisco.
answer_options.D: Both accounts are secondhand because they were written long after the bridge was built.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because Account 1 is written from a personal perspective using first-person pronouns ('I', 'my') and describes specific sensory details and emotions experienced by the narrator (the fog, wobbly legs, the father's words), which identifies it as a firsthand account. Account 2 provides general information, facts, and statistics about the bridge (cost, length, purpose) from an outside observer's perspective, which identifies it as a secondhand account.
subject: ELA

question:
Scientists are studying a population of bald eagles in a national park to see if conservation efforts are helping the birds thrive. Each winter, the scientists count the number of eagle nests in the park. The chart below shows their data from 2018 to 2022.

**Chart: Eagle Nests in the Park**
- 2018: 20 nests
- 2019: 35 nests
- 2020: 50 nests
- 2021: 65 nests
- 2022: 80 nests

Which choice describes how the information in the chart contributes to the reader's understanding of the text?

answer_options.A: It shows that the number of eagle nests decreased each winter.
answer_options.B: It shows that the number of eagle nests stayed the same each winter.
answer_options.C: It shows that the number of eagle nests increased each winter.
answer_options.D: It shows that scientists counted only 20 nests in total across all five years.

answer_explanation: Option C is correct because the chart data shows a clear upward trend, with the number of nests rising from 20 in 2018 to 80 in 2022. This quantitative information helps the reader understand that the eagle population is growing, which supports the text's focus on whether conservation efforts are helping the eagles thrive.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage and examine the line graph data described below.

**Passage:**
Dr. Elena Morales has spent the last five years monitoring wildlife in Pine Ridge Forest. In her recent report to the state conservation board, she warns that the red-tailed hawk population is in "steep decline" and may disappear from the area within a decade if current trends continue.

**Line Graph Data: Annual Red-Tailed Hawk Nests in Pine Ridge Forest**
*   **2019:** 52 nests
*   **2020:** 41 nests
*   **2021:** 33 nests
*   **2022:** 24 nests
*   **2023:** 15 nests

Which choice best explains how the information in the line graph contributes to an understanding of Dr. Morales's warning?

answer_options.A: It proves that the hawks will definitely disappear by the year 2033.
answer_options.B: It shows the exact number of hawks living in the forest each year.
answer_options.C: It illustrates the steady downward trend in nest counts, providing quantitative evidence for the "steep decline" mentioned in the text.
answer_options.D: It indicates that the counting method changed each year, explaining why the numbers vary.

answer_explanation: Option C is correct because it accurately describes how the quantitative data supports the text. The line graph shows a consistent numerical decrease in nest counts over five years (from 52 to 15), which directly visualizes and substantiates the author's qualitative claim of a "steep decline" in the population, thereby strengthening the reader's understanding of the severity and urgency of Dr. Morales's warning.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage and the chart below.

**Passage:**
Marine biologist Dr. Lee has been monitoring the coral reefs near Key West. In her report, she notes that the reefs are 'bleaching'—losing their vibrant colors and turning white. She believes this change is happening faster than expected, which could harm the entire ocean ecosystem.

**Chart: Percentage of Healthy Coral**
| Year | Percentage of Healthy Coral |
|------|-----------------------------|
| 2019 | 80%                         |
| 2020 | 65%                         |
| 2021 | 45%                         |
| 2022 | 25%                         |

Which choice best explains how the information in the chart contributes to an understanding of Dr. Lee's observations?

answer_options.A: It shows that the coral reefs were fully recovered by the end of the study.
answer_options.B: It proves that warmer ocean water was the main cause of the bleaching.
answer_options.C: It demonstrates that the coral was losing health rapidly over the four-year period.
answer_options.D: It indicates that Dr. Lee only measured the coral during the winter months.

answer_explanation: Option C is correct because the quantitative data in the chart shows a steep decline in healthy coral from 80% to 25% over four years. This downward trend directly supports Dr. Lee's observation that the reefs are bleaching 'faster than expected,' helping the reader understand the severity and speed of the coral's deterioration described in the passage.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage below.

"Reading aloud to children every night is one of the best habits parents can build. When parents read aloud, children hear new words that expand their vocabulary. Additionally, sharing stories creates a special bond between parent and child that lasts a lifetime."

The author claims that reading aloud to children is beneficial. Which choice explains how the author supports this point?

answer_options.A: By describing different types of children's books
answer_options.B: By giving reasons such as vocabulary growth and family bonding
answer_options.C: By telling a story about one specific family
answer_options.D: By listing the names of famous authors

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because the author supports the point by providing specific reasons—explaining that reading aloud expands children's vocabulary and creates a special bond between parent and child.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage below.

"Many people believe that homework helps students learn, but too much homework can actually harm student success. First, excessive homework causes stress. When students spend hours on assignments every night, they lose sleep and feel anxious, which makes it harder to concentrate in class. Second, too much homework reduces time for important activities like playing sports or spending time with family. These activities teach social skills and teamwork that are also essential for success. Finally, studies show that in countries where students receive less homework, test scores are often higher than in countries with heavy homework loads. For example, students in Finland do less homework than American students but consistently rank among the top in international math and science tests."

The author claims that excessive homework negatively affects students' health and ability to learn. Which choice best explains how the author uses evidence to support this specific point?

answer_options.A: The author compares test scores in Finland and the United States to show that less homework leads to better academic results.
answer_options.B: The author mentions playing sports and family time to argue that social skills are essential for student success.
answer_options.C: The author explains that losing sleep and feeling anxious makes it harder for students to concentrate in class.
answer_options.D: The author states that many people believe homework helps students learn.

answer_explanation: Option C is correct because the author uses the specific consequences of sleep loss and anxiety as evidence to demonstrate the negative impact on student health, and connects these effects to difficulty concentrating in class to show how homework harms learning ability, thereby directly supporting the point about health and ability to learn.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage below.

"Breakfast is the most important meal of the day for students. Eating a healthy breakfast helps children concentrate better in class. A study of 5,000 students found that those who ate breakfast scored 15% higher on math tests than those who skipped it. Schools should therefore offer free breakfast to all students."

The author argues that breakfast is the most important meal of the day for students. Which choice explains how the author supports this point?

answer_options.A: By listing different types of breakfast foods like cereal and fruit
answer_options.B: By explaining why some students skip breakfast in the morning
answer_options.C: By presenting results from a study about student test scores
answer_options.D: By describing what time school cafeterias open

answer_explanation: Option C is correct because the author supports the point that breakfast is important for students by presenting specific results from a study of 5,000 students, which provides factual evidence showing that students who ate breakfast scored higher on math tests than those who skipped it.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the two passages about how animals help plants.

**Passage 1: Honeybees at Work**
When a honeybee lands on a flower, it uses its long tongue to sip nectar. While it feeds, sticky pollen grains catch on the bee’s hairy legs and body. When the bee visits the next flower, some of this pollen brushes off, helping the plant make seeds.

**Passage 2: Monarch Butterflies**
Monarch butterflies feed on bright flowers to drink nectar. As a butterfly probes a flower with its thin mouthpart, powdery pollen sticks to its legs and wings. When the butterfly flutters to another flower of the same kind, it carries this pollen along, allowing the plants to produce seeds.

Which choice describes what both honeybees and monarch butterflies do to help plants reproduce?

answer_options.A: They use sharp teeth to break open hard seeds.
answer_options.B: They carry pollen between flowers while feeding on nectar.
answer_options.C: They bury seeds in the soil to grow new plants.
answer_options.D: They fan the flowers with their wings to spread the scent.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because Passage 1 states that pollen catches on the bee’s hairy body and brushes off at the next flower, and Passage 2 states that pollen sticks to the butterfly’s legs and wings and is carried to another flower. Both texts describe the animals transferring pollen between flowers while they feed on nectar, which helps the plants make seeds.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the two passages about animals and their environments.

**Passage 1: The Arctic Fox**
The Arctic fox lives in cold regions where snow covers the ground for much of the year. During winter, its thick fur turns pure white, allowing it to blend in with the snow. When summer arrives and the snow melts, the fox’s fur changes to brown or gray so it matches the rocks and tundra around it. This color change helps the fox hide from predators and sneak up on its prey.

**Passage 2: The Walking Stick Insect**
The walking stick insect lives in forests and meadows where trees and bushes grow. Its body is long, thin, and brown or green, making it look exactly like a small twig. When the insect rests on a plant, it stays perfectly still. Because it looks so much like part of the plant, birds and other predators often cannot see it at all.

Based on information from both texts, which choice best describes how both the Arctic fox and the walking stick insect survive?

answer_options.A: They both use speed to outrun their enemies.
answer_options.B: They both use sharp teeth or claws to fight predators.
answer_options.C: They both use body covering or shape to blend into their surroundings.
answer_options.D: They both migrate to warmer places during winter.

answer_explanation: Option C is correct because Passage 1 explains that the Arctic fox changes its fur color to match the snow or rocks, and Passage 2 describes how the walking stick insect has a body shaped like a twig. Both texts show that these animals use their physical appearance—fur color for the fox and body shape for the insect—to blend into their environments and avoid being seen by predators.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the two passages about desert life.

**Passage 1: The Fennec Fox**
The fennec fox lives in the hot Sahara Desert. This small fox has oversized ears that help spread heat away from its body. It also has thick fur on the bottoms of its feet to protect them from the burning hot sand.

**Passage 2: The Saguaro Cactus**
The saguaro cactus grows in the dry Sonoran Desert. This tall plant has shallow roots that spread out wide to catch as much rain as possible. Its thick, waxy stem stores water to help it survive long dry periods.

Based on information from both passages, which choice describes what the fennec fox and the saguaro cactus have in common?

answer_options.A: They both store food in their cheeks for later.
answer_options.B: They both have body parts that help them survive extreme heat and dryness.
answer_options.C: They both hunt at night to avoid the hot sun.
answer_options.D: They both lose their leaves every winter to save water.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because it integrates the key details from both passages. Passage 1 explains that the fennec fox has large ears to release heat and fur to protect its feet from hot sand. Passage 2 explains that the saguaro cactus has shallow roots to catch rain and a thick stem to store water. Both texts show that these organisms have specific physical adaptations that allow them to survive in hot, dry desert environments.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the two texts below about squirrels.

**Text 1**
In the autumn, squirrels collect nuts and bury them in the ground. They save these nuts to eat later when food is hard to find.

**Text 2**
During winter, snow covers the ground, making it difficult to find fresh food. Squirrels dig through the snow to uncover the nuts they buried earlier.

Based on both texts, which choice describes how squirrels get food during winter?

answer_options.A: By climbing trees to find fresh leaves
answer_options.B: By digging up nuts they buried in the fall
answer_options.C: By sleeping through the winter without eating
answer_options.D: By finding nuts buried by other animals

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because Text 1 explains that squirrels bury nuts in the ground during autumn to store them for later, and Text 2 explains that during winter squirrels dig through the snow to find these buried nuts. Integrating these details shows that squirrels survive winter by digging up the nuts they previously buried.
subject: ELA

question:
**Text 1:** In April 1815, Mount Tambora in Indonesia erupted violently, sending massive clouds of sulfur and ash high into Earth's atmosphere. Scientists later discovered that this ash spread around the globe and acted like a shield, blocking some of the sun's warmth from reaching the surface for many months.

**Text 2:** During the summer of 1816, farmers in New England were shocked when snow fell in June and frost killed their corn and wheat crops in July. Food became scarce, many families went hungry, and historians later called that terrible season "The Year Without a Summer."

Which choice best explains why New England farmers suffered crop failures during the summer of 1816?

answer_options.A: Thick volcanic ash from Mount Tambora physically covered the fields in New England and smothered the plants.
answer_options.B: The volcanic ash from Mount Tambora blocked sunlight, causing the cold temperatures that destroyed the crops.
answer_options.C: Farmers planted their seeds too early in the spring before the soil was warm enough to support growth.
answer_options.D: Snow and frost during June and July are typical weather patterns for New England that often ruin harvests.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because it integrates the scientific mechanism described in Text 1—that volcanic ash blocked sunlight—with the agricultural consequence described in Text 2—that cold temperatures destroyed crops. By synthesizing these details from both texts, the answer establishes the causal relationship between the distant volcanic eruption and the local farming disaster.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the two texts about honeybees and flowers.

**Text 1**
Honeybees visit flowers to collect nectar for making honey. As a bee crawls inside a flower, tiny grains of pollen stick to its fuzzy legs and body.

**Text 2**
When a honeybee flies to another flower of the same type, the pollen grains rub off its body onto the female part of that flower. This pollen transfer allows the flower to produce seeds and grow fruit.

Which choice best explains how honeybees help flowers produce fruit, using information from both texts?

answer_options.A: They collect nectar from flowers to make honey.
answer_options.B: They carry pollen on their bodies from one flower to another.
answer_options.C: They rub pollen onto the female part of flowers.
answer_options.D: They fly to find flowers that have the most nectar.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because it integrates information from both texts: Text 1 explains that bees pick up pollen on their bodies and legs, while Text 2 explains that this pollen is carried to other flowers and rubs off, enabling the flowers to produce fruit. This choice combines the mechanism of transport from Text 1 with the action of transfer from Text 2 to fully explain the process.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the following passage and answer the question.

Maria opened her lunch bag and sighed. She saw a squashed sandwich and a warm juice box inside. She remembered that she had left the bag on the kitchen counter all morning while she played outside in the yard.

Which choice tells where Maria left her lunch bag all morning?

answer_options.A: In her school locker
answer_options.B: On the playground
answer_options.C: On the kitchen counter
answer_options.D: Inside the refrigerator

answer_explanation: Option C is correct because the text explicitly states that Maria "had left the bag on the kitchen counter all morning while she played outside in the yard."
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage and answer the question.

Javier rearranged the pencils in his desk drawer for the third time, aligning them by shade from light yellow to dark umber. The art show judging had ended an hour ago, yet the certificate remained folded in his back pocket, unread. When his sister called up the stairs to ask about dinner, he told her he wasn't hungry. He picked up his phone, scrolled to his mother's contact, then set it down without typing. Instead, he walked to the window and watched the neighbor's dog chase a squirrel across the lawn, his fingers tracing slow circles on the glass. The sketchbook on his bed remained closed, its new leather cover catching the afternoon light.

Which choice best describes what can be inferred about Javier based on the details in the text?

answer_options.A: He is excited to show his mother the certificate he won at the art show.
answer_options.B: He is disappointed about the outcome of the art show judging.
answer_options.C: He is preparing to start a new sketching project using the neighbor's dog as a model.
answer_options.D: He is angry at his sister for interrupting his work.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because multiple textual details support the inference that Javier is experiencing disappointment. The passage explicitly states that he left the certificate unread in his pocket rather than examining it, avoided calling his mother despite having his phone ready, and refused dinner when his sister offered. His repetitive organizing of pencils, staring out the window at the dog, and leaving his new sketchbook closed further suggest he is avoiding engagement with art and distracting himself from upsetting news, behaviors consistent with processing an unfavorable outcome at the art show.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage and answer the question.

When the library bell rang, Marcus didn't head toward the exit with the other students. Instead, he walked to the far corner where the reference books were kept. He pulled out his notebook, which was filled with lists of ship names and dates. Mrs. Chen, the librarian, had left a thick envelope on the desk with his name on it. Marcus smiled when he saw the word "Archives" stamped in red on the front.

Which choice best describes what Marcus is most likely preparing to do?

answer_options.A: Check out a novel about sailing adventures
answer_options.B: Research information about ships
answer_options.C: Ask Mrs. Chen for help finding the exit
answer_options.D: Organize the reference books as a volunteer

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because the text explicitly states that Marcus's notebook contains 'lists of ship names and dates,' and he receives an envelope from the 'Archives' while standing in the reference section. These details indicate he is gathering historical information about ships.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the paragraph below.

"Every morning before school, Jin makes sure his dog, Baxter, has fresh water and a full bowl of food. He also takes Baxter for a short walk around the block. 'Taking care of Baxter is my responsibility,' Jin tells his mother."

Which choice from the passage explicitly shows what Jin does to care for Baxter each morning?

answer_options.A: He gives Baxter a bath.
answer_options.B: He takes Baxter to the vet.
answer_options.C: He takes Baxter for a short walk around the block.
answer_options.D: He plays fetch with Baxter in the park.

answer_explanation: Option C is correct because the text explicitly states that Jin 'takes Baxter for a short walk around the block' as part of his morning routine to care for his dog.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage below.

Maya was determined to win the school spelling bee. For three weeks, she studied every night using flashcards her older brother had made. She practiced by writing each difficult word ten times on lined paper. On the morning of the competition, Maya packed her lucky pencil and a bottle of water in her backpack. She arrived at the auditorium fifteen minutes early and sat in the front row, reviewing her notes silently until her name was called.

Which choice provides a specific detail from the passage that explicitly explains what Maya did to prepare for the spelling bee?

answer_options.A: She felt confident about winning because she was naturally smart.
answer_options.B: She practiced by writing each difficult word ten times on lined paper.
answer_options.C: She arrived early to find a good seat near the judges.
answer_options.D: She used flashcards to quiz her brother on spelling words.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because the passage explicitly states that Maya 'practiced by writing each difficult word ten times on lined paper,' which is a specific detail describing her preparation method for the spelling bee.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage below.

Marcus stared at the blank page in front of him. His pencil sat untouched on the desk while he tapped his fingers against the wood. Every few seconds, he glanced at the clock on the wall and sighed. When his teacher asked if he needed help starting his story, Marcus admitted he couldn't decide which topic to choose.

Which choice from the passage explicitly explains why Marcus was not writing?

answer_options.A: He was waiting for his pencil to sharpen.
answer_options.B: He could not decide which topic to choose for his story.
answer_options.C: He was bored with the assignment.
answer_options.D: He was watching the clock to know when class ended.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because the passage explicitly states that Marcus admitted he 'couldn't decide which topic to choose' when his teacher asked if he needed help starting his story, which directly explains why he was not writing.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the paragraph below.

Maya's hands trembled as she clutched the crumpled speech in her pocket. She stared at the heavy oak doors of the school auditorium, then checked her watch for the third time in one minute. Taking a deep breath, she smoothed her wrinkled shirt and walked slowly toward the entrance. The muffled sound of applause drifted through the walls. Maya paused, wiped her sweaty palms on her skirt, and whispered the first line of her speech one more time.

Which choice best describes how Maya is probably feeling?

answer_options.A: Angry that she arrived late
answer_options.B: Nervous about speaking in public
answer_options.C: Excited to watch the performance
answer_options.D: Tired from writing her speech

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because the text provides specific details that support the inference that Maya feels nervous: her hands "trembled," she checked her watch repeatedly, she took a "deep breath," she wiped her "sweaty palms," and she rehearsed her speech. These examples indicate anxiety about performing rather than anger, excitement, or fatigue.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage below.

Jasper's fingers hovered over the keyboard, the cursor blinking steadily in the empty search bar. The library around him had emptied hours ago, but he remained in the carrel by the window, the sunset turning the pages of his open notebook orange. He had copied the same web address three times into the margins, each time crossing it out with a darker, heavier line. When the janitor's cart rattled in the distant hallway, Jasper closed the laptop quickly, but then reopened it, staring at the blank email draft addressed to the university admissions office. He reached into his pocket and pulled out a crumpled envelope—the one his coach had given him that morning—and smoothed it against his knee without opening it. Outside, the streetlights flickered on, casting long shadows across his unfinished homework.

Which choice best describes what can be inferred about Jasper's situation based on the details in the text?

answer_options.A: Jasper is nervous about sending an important email to his teacher.
answer_options.B: Jasper has already been accepted to the university and is declining the offer.
answer_options.C: Jasper is torn between two different future paths and is afraid to take the next step.
answer_options.D: Jasper is worried that he will get in trouble for staying late at the library.

answer_explanation: Option C is correct because the text provides multiple details showing Jasper's internal conflict: he has a blank email draft addressed to the university admissions office, indicating an academic path he has not pursued, while simultaneously holding an unopened envelope from his coach that likely represents an athletic opportunity. The detail of him copying and heavily crossing out the same web address three times demonstrates hesitation and fear of commitment, and the fact that he stays in the empty library for hours suggests prolonged indecision rather than a single task.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage.

Jenna stood backstage, clutching the frayed hem of her sweater until her knuckles turned white. The auditorium lights hummed above her, and she could hear the murmur of the crowd beyond the heavy velvet curtain. She had practiced her piano piece for three months, yet her fingers felt stiff and cold as she flexed them. Her water bottle sat untouched on the folding table. When her name echoed through the speaker, Jenna closed her eyes and counted to five. Then, squaring her shoulders, she walked toward the stage.

Which detail from the passage best supports the inference that Jenna is nervous before her performance?

answer_options.A: She had practiced her piano piece for three months.
answer_options.B: Her fingers felt stiff and cold as she flexed them.
answer_options.C: The auditorium lights hummed above her.
answer_options.D: She walked toward the stage.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because the description of Jenna's fingers feeling stiff and cold provides specific physical evidence of nervousness; this physiological reaction is a text detail that directly supports the inference that she is anxious about her upcoming performance.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story below.

The Hidden Garden

Marcus moved to a new neighborhood in the middle of summer. Everything felt unfamiliar—the tall fences, the winding streets, and the quiet houses. One afternoon, while exploring behind his garage, he noticed a loose board in the fence. He pushed it aside and discovered a small, overgrown garden no one seemed to visit. Weeds covered the ground, but Marcus spotted a few struggling tomato plants trying to grow through the thick grass. Each day after that, Marcus brought a watering can and carefully pulled out the weeds. By August, the garden was full of ripe red tomatoes. Marcus shared the harvest with his neighbors, and soon the hidden garden became a gathering place where families met and talked.

Which choice best explains why Marcus was able to turn the garden into a place where neighbors gathered?

answer_options.A: He built a new fence to replace the loose board.
answer_options.B: He found a gardening book that taught him special tricks.
answer_options.C: He worked every day to care for the plants and then shared the food.
answer_options.D: He asked his parents to hire a gardener to clean the space.

answer_explanation: Option C is correct because the story states that Marcus brought a watering can and pulled weeds 'each day' to care for the struggling plants, and later 'shared the harvest with his neighbors,' which directly led to the garden becoming a gathering place for the community.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story below.

Mira stood at the edge of the community garden, watching the new neighbor, Mr. Henderson, tend to a plot that had been abandoned since summer. The soil was cracked and gray, yet he worked slowly, turning it with a spade as if coaxing something hidden to the surface. Other neighbors had complained about the eyesore, petitioning the city to pave it over for parking. But Mr. Henderson didn't rush. He added compost not just to his own plot, but to the surrounding soil that belonged to no one. When children dropped trash near the fence, he didn't scold them; he simply picked it up and continued kneading the earth between his fingers. By October, when the frost threatened, his plot wasn't the only thing blooming. Wildflowers had sprouted along the fence line where he had scattered seeds, and the cracked soil had softened to brown velvet.

Which choice best describes what the story suggests about Mr. Henderson's understanding of community?

answer_options.A: He believes problems should be solved quickly through official city action.
answer_options.B: He understands that care given to neglected spaces can spread beyond boundaries.
answer_options.C: He thinks children should be punished for littering in shared areas.
answer_options.D: He prefers working alone rather than interacting with his neighbors.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because the text describes Mr. Henderson adding compost to soil beyond his own plot and scattering seeds along the fence line, actions which lead to wildflowers blooming and the surrounding earth softening. This demonstrates his belief that patient, individual care for neglected areas creates positive change that extends past personal property lines to benefit the wider community.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story below.

Marcus's father had warned him about the old lighthouse. "It's been dark for twenty years," he'd said. "The coast guard uses satellites now."

Still, Marcus pedaled his bike to the rocky point every Saturday. He liked the way the wind tasted like salt and secrets. One afternoon, he found the door unlocked. Inside, dust covered the brass instruments, but the spiral staircase was swept clean.

On the observation deck, an old man polished the glass of the massive lamp. "My grandfather kept this light," the man said without turning. "I couldn't let it go completely dark."

Marcus helped oil the gears. When autumn storms cut the town's power, the old lamp cast its beam across the harbor, guiding the fishing fleet home.

Which choice best describes the central message about tradition that the story conveys?

answer_options.A: Modern technology always replaces old methods completely.
answer_options.B: Abandoned buildings should be avoided for safety reasons.
answer_options.C: Maintaining family traditions can provide unexpected benefits.
answer_options.D: Electronic navigation systems are always reliable during storms.

answer_explanation: Option C is correct because the story shows the old man maintaining the lighthouse tradition despite modern alternatives, and this traditional care proves valuable when it guides the fishing fleet home during a power outage, demonstrating that keeping family traditions can have practical, unexpected benefits.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage.

Maya loved basketball, but she was the shortest player on the team. During the first game, she missed every shot she took. The other team laughed when she tried to block their tall players. After the game, Maya wanted to quit.

Her coach sat down beside her. "Height helps, but practice helps more," he said.

Every morning for three weeks, Maya woke up early to shoot baskets in her driveway. Her arms ached and her legs were sore, but she kept going.

At the next game, Maya still couldn't reach the tallest players. But when the ball came to her, she dribbled quickly around the defense. Her shot arced through the air and swished through the net. The crowd cheered. Maya smiled, knowing that her hard work had paid off.

Which choice best states the theme of the story?

answer_options.A: Being tall is necessary to play basketball well.
answer_options.B: Hard work can help you overcome challenges.
answer_options.C: Coaches always know the best way to win games.
answer_options.D: Basketball is the most difficult sport to learn.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because the story shows Maya practicing every day despite being short and missing shots, which leads to her successfully making a basket later, demonstrating that consistent effort helps overcome difficulties.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage below.

Maya sat on the porch, her fingers cramped around the reeds. The basket she was weaving looked lopsided, with gaps where the strands refused to meet. She threw it down. "I'll never get it right like you, Grandma," she muttered.

Grandma didn't pick up the basket. Instead, she reached for her own half-finished work. "See this bulge? Yesterday I had to undo three rows." She pointed to a dark stain on her apron. "Last week, I dyed these reeds the wrong color—mud brown instead of amber. I used them anyway for the garden basket."

Maya stared. She had assumed Grandma's hands just knew what to do. "You mean you still mess up?"

"Every piece," Grandma laughed. "The difference isn't the mistakes. It's what you do after you make them."

Maya picked up her lopsided basket. The gaps were still there, but she found the next reed and threaded it through.

Which choice best expresses a theme of the story?

answer_options.A: Grandmother teaches Maya how to weave perfect baskets using traditional methods.
answer_options.B: Maya learns that even experienced artisans make mistakes while working.
answer_options.C: Family traditions are most valuable when passed down through generations.
answer_options.D: Developing skill requires continuing to work through errors and frustration.

answer_explanation: Option D is correct because the story develops this theme through specific details: Grandmother reveals her own weaving imperfections to demonstrate that mistakes are inevitable, advises Maya that 'the difference isn't the mistakes, it's what you do after,' and Maya ultimately chooses to continue her lopsided basket despite her earlier frustration. These details collectively support the universal idea that mastery emerges from persistence through failure rather than from innate talent or flawless execution.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage below.

Maya loved painting, but her hands shook when she held the brush. At the school art fair, she watched other students paint beautiful landscapes while her canvas stayed blank. "I can't do this," she whispered, tears forming. Her art teacher, Ms. Chen, knelt beside her. "Every artist started with shaky lines," Ms. Chen said. "The important part is beginning again." Maya dipped her brush. Her first tree looked lumpy, but she painted another. By the end of the afternoon, her forest wasn't perfect, but it was hers. She hung it proudly, knowing she had finished what she started.

Which choice best expresses a theme of the story?

answer_options.A: Art teachers are the most helpful people in schools.
answer_options.B: Hard work and persistence lead to success.
answer_options.C: School art fairs are fun events for students.
answer_options.D: Painting landscapes is difficult for beginners.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because the story illustrates how Maya continues painting despite her initial struggles and imperfect first attempts. Her persistence in beginning again and finishing the painting demonstrates that hard work and determination lead to personal success and pride.
subject: ELA

question:
The rain poured down on the soccer field, turning the grass into thick mud. Marcus looked up at the dark clouds and squeezed his soccer ball tightly. "We can't give up now," he said loudly to his teammates. "We've practiced every day for three weeks to get here." As the storm grew stronger, Marcus did not run for the sidelines. He dribbled the ball through the puddles and shouted encouragement to others. Even when he slipped and fell into the mud, he jumped back up and kept sprinting until the final whistle blew.

Which choice best describes Marcus based on his words and actions in the story?

answer_options.A: Lazy and unmotivated
answer_options.B: Nervous and hesitant
answer_options.C: Determined and dedicated
answer_options.D: Careless and reckless

answer_explanation: Option C is correct because Marcus demonstrates determination and dedication through specific details in the text. He tells his team, "We can't give up now," and references that they "practiced every day for three weeks," showing his commitment to the game. His actions further support this trait: he refuses to head for the sidelines during the storm, continues dribbling through puddles, and gets back up after falling to keep playing until the final whistle, all indicating he is dedicated to finishing the match despite the difficult conditions.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage below.

The auditorium buzzed with nervous energy as the fourth-grade orchestra tuned their instruments. Mrs. Chen marched down the aisle, her sharp heels clicking against the floor. "That violin is out of tune," she snapped at Leo, making him jump. "Play it again from measure twelve, and this time, count aloud." When Leo stumbled through the notes a second time, she tapped her baton sharply against her music stand and frowned. During the break, however, she knelt beside Leo's chair and adjusted his shoulder rest with gentle hands. "Your fingers know the pattern," she whispered, "but your mind is rushing ahead. Trust your preparation."

Which choice best describes Mrs. Chen based on specific details from the beginning and end of the passage?

answer_options.A: Impatient and unsatisfied
answer_options.B: Strict but encouraging
answer_options.C: Anxious about the performance
answer_options.D: Disappointed by Leo's errors

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because the text presents Mrs. Chen as maintaining high standards when she sharply corrects Leo's tuning and frowns at his stumbling, while simultaneously revealing her supportive nature through her gentle physical assistance and whispered advice to "trust your preparation." These specific contrasting details from her words and actions demonstrate that she combines strict expectations with genuine encouragement.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage below.

Marcus stood at the edge of the diving board, staring down at the blue water twelve feet below.
